title: lastrea oligophlebia var. subtripinnata
intro: Lastrea oligophlebia var. subtripinnata, commonly known as the subtripinnate lady fern, is a plant species belonging to the family Athyriaceae. This plant is valued for its traditional use in folk medicine, where it is believed to possess various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The therapeutic actions of Lastrea oligophlebia var. subtripinnata include wound healing, antiseptic, and anti-allergic activities, which are attributed to its bioactive constituents, such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides. Further research is required to confirm the efficacy and safety of this plant for medicinal use.

side_effects: Lastrea oligophlebia var. subtripinnata can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory problems.
